precalculus-2e:c1s1.reason: Function notation builds on Algebra 2 relations/functions and graphs.

precalculus-2e:c1s2.reason: Domain and range are properties of functions.

precalculus-2e:c1s3.reason: Rates of change and graph behavior need function graphs.

precalculus-2e:c1s4.reason: Composition continues inverse/composition ideas from Algebra 2.

precalculus-2e:c1s6.reason: Absolute-value functions use transformations and absolute-value inequalities.

precalculus-2e:c1s7.reason: Inverse functions formalize one-to-one composition ideas.

precalculus-2e:c2s1.reason: Linear functions revisit line equations in function language.

precalculus-2e:c2s3.reason: Linear modeling uses linear functions and their graphs.

precalculus-2e:c2s4.reason: Fitting linear models extends linear modeling.

precalculus-2e:c3s1.reason: Complex numbers continue Algebra 2 complex-number work.

precalculus-2e:c3s2.reason: Quadratic functions deepen Algebra 2 quadratic graphs with transformations.

precalculus-2e:c3s3.reason: Polynomial functions extend quadratics and polynomial operations.

precalculus-2e:c3s6.reason: Zeros of polynomials use graphs, division, and complex numbers.

precalculus-2e:c3s7.reason: Rational functions need polynomials and rational expressions.

precalculus-2e:c3s8.reason: Inverse and radical functions combine inverses with radicals.

precalculus-2e:c4s2.reason: Exponential graphs use exponential definitions and transformations.

precalculus-2e:c4s4.reason: Log graphs use log definitions and transformations.

precalculus-2e:c4s5.reason: Log properties continue Algebra 2 log properties.

precalculus-2e:c4s8.reason: Fitting exponential models extends modeling and linear-fit ideas.

precalculus-2e:c5s1.reason: Angles in standard position build on geometry trig angle measure.

precalculus-2e:c5s2.reason: Unit-circle sine/cosine define trig as functions of angles.

precalculus-2e:c5s3.reason: Other trig functions are ratios built from sine and cosine.

precalculus-2e:c5s4.reason: Right-triangle trig connects unit-circle values to triangle ratios.

precalculus-2e:c6s1.reason: Sine/cosine graphs use unit-circle functions and transformations.

precalculus-2e:c6s2.reason: Other trig graphs extend sine/cosine graphing.

precalculus-2e:c6s3.reason: Inverse trig needs trig functions and inverses.

precalculus-2e:c7s2.reason: Sum/difference identities build on basic identity fluency.

precalculus-2e:c7s3.reason: Double/half-angle formulas derive from sum identities.

precalculus-2e:c7s4.reason: Product-sum formulas are sibling identities to sum/difference.

precalculus-2e:c7s5.reason: Trig equations use identities and inverse trig.

precalculus-2e:c7s6.reason: Trig modeling uses graphs and equation solving.

precalculus-2e:c8s1.reason: Law of Sines extends right-triangle trig to general triangles.

precalculus-2e:c8s2.reason: Law of Cosines is another non-right-triangle tool alongside Law of Sines.

precalculus-2e:c8s3.reason: Polar coordinates reinterpret plane points using angles.

precalculus-2e:c8s4.reason: Polar graphs use polar coordinates and periodic intuition.

precalculus-2e:c8s5.reason: Polar form of complex numbers uses polar coords and complexes.

precalculus-2e:c8s6.reason: Parametric equations describe plane curves with two functions.

precalculus-2e:c8s8.reason: Vectors use directed magnitude and trig direction.

precalculus-2e:c9s3.reason: Nonlinear systems continue Algebra 2 nonlinear systems.

precalculus-2e:c9s6.reason: Gaussian elimination uses matrices on multi-variable systems.

precalculus-2e:c9s7.reason: Matrix inverses solve systems after matrix operations.

precalculus-2e:c10s1.reason: Ellipses continue Algebra 2 conics with transformations.

precalculus-2e:c10s3.reason: Parabolas as conics connect quadratic and analytic forms.

precalculus-2e:c10s5.reason: Polar conics combine conics with polar coordinates.

precalculus-2e:c11s4.reason: Series sum arithmetic and geometric sequences.

precalculus-2e:c11s5.reason: Counting principles extend geometry probability counting.

precalculus-2e:c11s6.reason: Binomial theorem uses counting and prior binomial work.

precalculus-2e:c12s1.reason: Numerical/graphical limits need function graphs and behavior.

precalculus-2e:c12s2.reason: Limit properties evaluate limits introduced graphically/numerically.

precalculus-2e:c12s4.reason: Derivatives are defined as limits of difference quotients.
